summary: Stellar Scarlet>Fire jam, half‑step Franklin's, tight second set
blurb: The show delivers a monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain sequence and a soaring Mississippi Half‑Step into Franklin’s Tower. Jerry’s solos are inventive throughout while Phil’s booming bass drives the jams. Highlights include Wharf Rat, Good Lovin’ and a spirited Big River.
review: On September 1, 1979 at Holleder Memorial Stadium the Dead turned a warm summer night into one of their most exhilarating late‑'70s outings. The first set rolls out with a crisp Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo that segues straight into Franklin’s Tower, setting up a high‑octane vibe that only grows louder in the second set. Scarlet Begonias erupts into an expansive Fire on the Mountain jam that many reviewers call “monster” – Jerry’s tone on his new ‘Tiger’ guitar soaring, weaving wah‑wah textures and blistering runs that keep the crowd hanging on every note. The half‑step transition into Franklin’s Tower is equally fluid, with Brent’s keys adding a sweet, supportive undercurrent while Phil’s bass thunders “inside your skull.”  The band rides this momentum through a tight Wharf Rat, an exuberant I Need A Miracle → Bertha → Good Lovin’ finale and a rousing encore of One More Saturday Night. Though Saint of Circumstance falters vocally for Bob, the overall performance is locked‑in, with Brent slaying on Big River and the dual drummers laying down a solid, propulsive foundation. Across multiple soundboard and audience tapes the consensus is clear: this show captures the Dead at a creative peak, delivering both the fireworks of extended jams and the intimacy of well‑crafted songcraft.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All nine recordings are marked positive, with multiple reviewers calling the performance "scorching", "epic" and "legendary"; standout songs (Scarlet>Fire, Half‑Step→Franklin's) receive repeated praise, and no consistent negative comments appear. This strong, uniform enthusiasm justifies a high sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Remastered soundboard with crystal‑clear, balanced mix that captures the band's dynamics and highlights the iconic Scarlet>Fire and half‑step Franklin's moments.

key_highlights: Monster Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain jam that dominates the second set
Soaring Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo into Franklin’s Tower showcases tight interplay
Brent Mydland’s tasteful keyboards, especially on Big River, add depth to the set
High energy despite curfew, ending with a spirited Good Lovin’ and One More Saturday Night

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers soaring, inventive solos – wah‑wah Fire on the Mountain and gorgeous half‑step Franklin’s tones – with only a minor timing slip in Wharf Rat.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass is booming and drives the jams, described as “inside your skull” and a catalyst that keeps the band happening throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals stumble on Saint of Circumstance but he shines on Good Lovin’ and adds enthusiastic rhythm guitar throughout the set.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland slays on keys, particularly adding flavor to Big River and supporting the extended jams with tasteful organ and piano work.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is solid and contributes clear, tight transitions, especially into the Scarlet>Fire space jam.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann provides a sturdy, propulsive foundation that underpins the high‑octane second set.
